description: Poison Ivy is a remote access tool, first released in 2005 but unchanged since 2008. It includes features common to most Windows-based RATs, including key logging, screen capturing, video capturing, file transfers, system administration, password theft, and traffic relaying.
name: Poison Ivy
summary:
The object contains information about a malware called Poison Ivy, which is classified as a remote access tool. It was first released in 2005 but has remained unchanged since 2008. The malware includes features common to most Windows-based RATs, such as key logging, screen capturing, video capturing, file transfers, system administration, password theft, and traffic relaying.

Poison Ivy is identified as a member of the remote-access-trojan malware type. This classification suggests that it was designed to provide unauthorized access to a victim's computer or network remotely. The information provided indicates that Poison Ivy includes various malicious capabilities that can be used by an attacker to compromise the security of a system.

description: Calamine is a set of free tools to help organizations detect and examine Poison Ivy infections on their systems. The package includes these components: PIVY callback-decoding tool (ChopShop Module) and PIVY memory-decoding tool (PIVY PyCommand Script).
external_references: [{"source_name": "Calamine ChopShop Module", "description": "The FireEye Poison Ivy decoder checks the beginning of each TCP session for possible PIVY challengeresponse sequences. If found, the module will try to validate the response using one or more passwords supplied as arguments.", "url": "https://github.com/fireeye/chopshop"}, {"source_name": "Calamine PyCommand Script", "description": "Helps locate the PIVY password.", "url": "https://github.com/fireeye/pycommands"}]
name: Analyze with FireEye Calamine Toolset
summary: The object contains a detailed description of the Calamine toolset used by organizations to detect and examine Poison Ivy infections on their systems. This set of tools includes two primary components, the PIVY callback-decoding tool (ChopShop Module) and the PIVY memory-decoding tool (PIVY PyCommand Script). The ChopShop Module is a FireEye-developed tool that checks the beginning of each TCP session for possible PIVY challenge-response sequences and validates them using one or more supplied passwords. On the other hand, the PIVY PyCommand Script is designed to help locate PIVY passwords. Both tools are open-source and can be found on GitHub repositories.

summary:
The object contains information about a vulnerability in a Microsoft Word document, specifically referencing CVE-2010-3333. This vulnerability was exploited through the use of weaponized documents to gain unauthorized access or disrupt systems. The description provided highlights the creative method used to take advantage of this flaw.

In terms of external validation, there is a single reference to the Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) database, which lists CVE-2010-3333 as an entry in its catalog. This suggests that the vulnerability has been documented and tracked by the CVE community.

Unfortunately, more detailed information about the nature of the vulnerability itself and the potential impact on systems is not available, as this data appears to be missing from the provided object.
